_meta.name: locator-drift
_meta.purpose: Claude classifies a locator-not-found failure as locator_drift with a machine_proposal — the canonical happy path for the auto-fix flow.
_meta.matches_on: prompt contains 'Locator not found' (from error_message in DiagnoseInput)

output.human_explanation: The 'login button' binding uses css '[data-testid="login-btn"]' but the live DOM exposes data-test-id (with hyphen) on the same element. The element exists; only the attribute name shifted. A minimum-diff rebind to the new attribute is safe.
evidence: Pre-failure DOM excerpt: <button data-test-id="login-btn" class="btn-primary">Sign in</button>
Binding declared at meta/Login.meta:14 with selector_strategy=css
Sibling scenario 'login with remembered creds' bound the same element via 'role text Sign in' and passed

alternatives_considered.rejected_because: DOM excerpt shows the button is present, only the attribute name changed
No async hydration window — the element is in the initial server-rendered HTML

machine_proposal.old: the login button can be located by css "[data-testid='login-btn']"
machine_proposal.new: the login button can be located by css "[data-test-id='login-btn']"
